purpose: Define what must be true before the replicated player-history signal from #112/#122 can ever be considered for production binding. Design/review artifact only; does not itself authorize any implementation.

triggering_decision.ceiling_note: Authorizes only opening this follow-up review/design issue (#123). Does not authorize production binding, feature wiring, seasonalPprModel.ts changes, or product output.

evidence_chain.attribution_note: production_only (joined MAE 40.173) within 0.138 of full_feature_set (40.034); production family (prior-year/trailing PPR) carries essentially all of the signal

production_binding_prerequisites.description: A versioned, non-breaking feature-contract identifier (see feature_contract_shape).
The production feature must pin to a specific promoted artifact path + sha256 + promotion review, fail closed on mismatch.
A production split/retraining-cadence policy must be defined and reviewed separately from the one-shot 2022-2024 -> 2025 experimental split.

A production-scoped leakage review broader than the experimental leakage discipline: real-time data availability at inference time, look-ahead in derived features, generated_at timing vs in-season availability.
A documented command sequence reproducing the cited numbers, exercised (not just read) by the reviewer.
Any production acceptance criterion must require beating both a baseline and a deterministic shuffled control, not baseline alone.
Not set by this document; a future issue must propose and get review for a concrete threshold.
Explicit, reviewed behavior for the no-history subgroup; never silently zero-filled.
If the source artifact becomes unavailable/stale/fails re-verification, the production path fails closed to current baseline behavior.
Any consumer (ranking/projection/advice surface) must be named, scoped, and separately approved.
No decision in this chain or this document authorizes automatic/unsupervised binding; every step requires a new issue and human-reviewed PR.

feature_contract_shape.note: Design proposal only. Not implemented as a type or schema consumed anywhere in this PR.

temporal_cutoff_semantics.rule: feature values for target season S are built ONLY from seasons < S
temporal_cutoff_semantics.input_window: rolling N prior seasons (N to be fixed by the future implementation issue; 3 in this experiment)
temporal_cutoff_semantics.excluded: target season S in any form, including partial-season in-progress data

feature_availability_rules.requires: at least one prior-season REG record for this player_id in the input window
feature_availability_rules.family_scope_note: production-family aggregates carry ~all of the observed signal per #116 attribution; a minimal contract may be scoped to production-only fields, subject to future review

null_missing_history_rules.no_history_player: entire feature block is null, never zero-filled, never imputed at serve time using post-hoc population statistics
null_missing_history_rules.unavailable_usage_fields: remain null even when history exists, exactly as in the source artifact

feature_contract_shape.generated_at_expectation: ISO-8601 timestamp of generation, not of consumption
feature_contract_shape.run_id_expectation: deterministic identifier tying a generated feature set to a specific source artifact sha256 + contract_version + generator script version
feature_contract_shape.non_advice_non_ranking_statement: This contract describes a candidate MODEL FEATURE only. It is not fantasy advice, not a ranking, not a start/sit recommendation, and not a product-facing claim. No consumer may present its values, or any derivative of them, as advice or ranking output without a separate, explicitly-approved product-integration review.

future_implementation_gates: design contract reviewed and explicitly accepted (not just drafted)
source artifact identity for the contract locked (path + sha256 + promotion review), with documented fail-closed re-verification
leakage audit passed for the specific production inference path proposed (broader than experimental leakage discipline)
deterministic rerun command exercised by the reviewer, not just read
validation for the production proposal uses real-vs-baseline-vs-shuffled framing on the proposal's actual target population
missing/invalid player-history failure mode documented for the specific production consumer proposed
no Fantasy/product consumer change included in the same slice as contract implementation
human reviewer with authority over seasonalPprModel.ts has explicitly signed off
non_goals: does not bind any feature into seasonalPprModel.ts or any other production path
does not set a production acceptance threshold (deferred to a future issue)
does not lock the feature-contract's source artifact sha256 (happens at contract-acceptance time, not yet occurred)
does not approve a Fantasy consumer, UI surface, ranking, or advice behavior of any kind
does not promote or demote any TIBER-Data artifact
does not claim the replicated result is stable across future seasons, future promoted-artifact refreshes, or a production-scale rolling retraining cadence

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future PR wires the feature directly into seasonalPprModel.ts citing replication alone
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: boundary_preserved + future_implementation_gates make explicit that replication alone satisfies none of the production-binding gates

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future PR locks a final contract without review
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: feature_contract_shape.validation_status is design_proposed_not_reviewed; contract acceptance is its own gate

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future PR silently zero-fills missing player history in production
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: null_missing_history_rules + missing_invalid_history_behavior prerequisite require explicit, reviewed behavior

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future PR bundles contract implementation with a Fantasy-facing consumer change
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: future_implementation_gates explicitly forbids bundling; consumer approval is a separate prerequisite

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future PR treats the promoted artifact as permanently trustworthy without re-verification
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: source_artifact_identity_and_hash_expectations prerequisite requires fail-closed re-verification, matching every gate in the chain

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future PR skips the shuffled-control framing once the signal looks obviously real
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: real_vs_baseline_vs_shuffled_validation_framing prerequisite and gate carry the framing forward

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future PR treats this document itself as authorization to proceed
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: purpose and boundary_preserved state plainly that this document authorizes nothing beyond its own existence

deterministic_replay_commands: npm run gate:player-history-promoted-source -- --artifact=<local promoted artifact> --manifest=<local promotion manifest>
npm run refresh:player-history-promoted-mirrors -- --artifact=<local promoted artifact> --manifest=<local promotion manifest>
next_step: Wait for a separate, explicitly-scoped implementation-design issue that addresses every future_implementation_gate before any code touching seasonalPprModel.ts or a production feature path is written. This document is that issue's prerequisite checklist, not that issue.
